extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 2.5km to go on 7km/7.1% Los de Gaud climb, bridged to GODDU's earlier move, dropped him in final kilometer for stage and yellow jersey
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD attacked first on Los de Gaud climb but POGAČAR marked easily, neutralizing move and forcing GC rivals to chase.
GODDU attacked 3.5km out forcing POGAČAR to bridge alone while VINGEGAARD failed to follow, revealing gulf in current form.
UAE Emirates chased breakaway all day despite no intermediate sprint threat, signaling aggressive stage-hunting intent rather than conservative GC racing.
INEOS led into final climb base then GALL took over briefly before attacks, showing team hierarchy confusion without clear protected rider.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: bridged 1km+ gap to GODDU solo then dropped him in final kilometer, showing superior form to July 2022 Vingegaard
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: initiated first attack but couldn't bridge when POGAČAR went to GODDU, losing 44 seconds suggests incomplete form preparation
rider_signals.GODDU David: positive: only rider besides POGAČAR to distance field with 3.5km attack, demanding Tour leadership with aggressive racing not words
rider_signals.GALL Felix: neutral: Austrian champion pulled for UAE on climb despite no team GC contender, suggests domestique role despite past stage race results
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: dropped when GODDU attacked 3.5km out, Bahrain's Gino MÄDER outperformed him significantly despite VLASOV's higher GC pedigree
